articles.title: Washington’s Volatility and Taipei’s Security
articles.title_zh: 华盛顿的波动与台北的安全
articles.description: Taiwan’s Defence Quandary Creates Openings for EuropeThe Trump-Xi summit in May 2026 highlighted a shift in Washington’s posture that introduces deep uncertainty into US-Taiwan relations. While the meeting was no turning-point, it underlined Taipei’s predicament: relying heavily on now question­able American promises. If Washington does reduce its security guarantees, Taiwan could strengthen its strategic alignment with Europe. That would present risks for Europe, but also opportunities.
articles.summary_zh: 2026年5月的特朗普-习近平峰会凸显了华盛顿立场的转变，为美台关系带来了深度不确定性。尽管此次会议并非转折点，但它凸显了台北的困境：严重依赖如今已存疑的美国承诺。如果华盛顿确实减少其安全保证，台湾可能会加强与欧洲的战略对齐。这对欧洲而言既带来风险，也提供了机遇。文章分析了台湾的防御困境如何为欧洲创造新的外交和安全空间，并探讨了欧洲在台海问题上的潜在角色与挑战。
subjects: Defence and security policy / Armed forces
Foreign policy of a country / a region
Defence and security policy / armed forces of a country / a region

articles.title: Peace in Crisis: Conflict Management in the Horn of Africa
articles.title_zh: 危机中的和平：非洲之角的冲突管理
articles.description: The system of international conflict management is in deep crisis. Relevant instruments such as UN peace operations are being withdrawn, normative and power-political preconditions (for example, US-guaranteed international order) no longer apply and all too often agreements have led to the entrenchment of violent authoritarian systems rather than their transformation. This crisis is particularly evident in the Horn of Africa. Neither peace agreements concluded under significant international pressure (as in the case of South Sudan) nor those negotiated between armed actors with virtually no mediation (as in Sudan in 2020) have brought about stability. Rather, governments (such as that of Ethiopia) have regularly exploited peace processes to secure their own rule. More recent mediation processes have been primarily a function of regional rivalries and the diplomatic interests of the external actors involved (as has been the case in Sudan since the current war began in April 2023). If foreign support is available, the parties to the conflict have fewer incentives to make compromises. Because mechanisms for implementing agreements are poorly resourced and receive little political support, there is no inclusive political process following ceasefires to address the root causes of the conflict. Sooner or later, fighting flares up once again. As far as European contributions to conflict resolution in the Horn of Africa are concerned, there are lessons to be learned from this crisis. Europe should neither cling nostalgically to what is a largely defunct system of conflict management, nor should it, out of frustration, adopt approaches dominated solely by security considerations. Instead, it should support civilian peace initiatives, help political economies of violence die out and rethink its own cooperation with governments that exacerbate conflict (such as that of the United Arab Emirates).
articles.summary_zh: 本文指出国际冲突管理体系正陷入深刻危机，联合国维和行动等工具被撤回，美国主导的国际秩序等规范与权力前提不再适用，和平协议往往巩固而非转变暴力威权体系。这一危机在非洲之角尤为明显：无论是国际压力下促成的南苏丹和平协议，还是2020年苏丹武装分子间几乎无调解的谈判，均未带来稳定。相反，埃塞俄比亚等国政府常利用和平进程巩固自身统治。近期调解更多受区域竞争和外部势力外交利益驱动（如2023年4月苏丹战争爆发后）。当外部支持可得时，冲突方妥协意愿降低；协议执行机制资源匮乏且缺乏政治支持，停火后缺乏包容性政治进程解决冲突根源，导致战火反复。文章建议欧洲不应怀旧地固守已失效的冲突管理体系，也不应因挫败而采取纯安全主导路径，而应支持民间和平倡议、推动暴力政治经济消亡，并重新审视与加剧冲突的政府（如阿联酋）的合作关系。

articles.title: Ambiguity in Ocean Governance
articles.description: The Advisory Opinion of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea on Climate ChangeIn January 2027, the first Conference of the Parties to the new United Nations High Seas Treaty will take place. The meeting is expected to address key conflicts between marine conservation and ocean use in the context of climate change. The 2024 ad­visory opinion of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS) on climate change is therefore once again attracting attention. Many observers had expected the opinion to clarify the relationship between ocean protection and utilisation in climate policy. Such clarification is particularly important in relation to emerging marine carbon dioxide removal (mCDR) technologies, which seek to enhance oceanic carbon drawdown in order to mitigate climate change. ITLOS confirmed that states are obliged to protect the ocean from pollution caused by atmospheric CO₂. However, it left open the question of whether mCDR should be regarded as marine pollution or as a contribution to marine environmental protection. This ambiguity could be mobilised politically to either promote or restrict mCDR. International organisations, EU institutions, and national authorities should therefore prepare for competing inter­pretations of the advisory opinion that may shape future climate and ocean governance.
articles.summary_zh: 2027年1月，联合国新的《公海条约》首次缔约方会议将召开，预计将讨论气候变化背景下海洋保护与利用之间的关键冲突。因此，2024年国际海洋法法庭（ITLOS）关于气候变化的咨询意见再次受到关注。许多观察者曾期望该意见能澄清气候政策中海洋保护与利用的关系，尤其是在新兴的海洋二氧化碳移除（mCDR）技术方面。ITLOS确认各国有义务保护海洋免受大气CO₂造成的污染，但未明确mCDR应被视为海洋污染还是对海洋环境保护的贡献。这种模糊性可能被政治利用，以促进或限制mCDR。国际组织、欧盟机构和国家当局应做好准备，应对该咨询意见可能出现的相互竞争的解释，这些解释将影响未来的气候和海洋治理。

articles.title: Untapped Potential at the European Public Prosecutor’s Office
articles.title_zh: 欧洲检察官办公室的未开发潜力：扩大职权以执行制裁
articles.description: Expand the Mandate to Include Sanctions EnforcementSanctions are a key instrument in the European Union’s foreign and security policy. Although their enforcement depends on the Union’s ability to act legally as well as politically, prosecutions remain constrained by national borders. For this reason, France and Germany proposed in 2022 that the mandate of the European Public Prosecutor’s Office (EPPO) be expanded to cover violations of EU sanctions. That initiative failed to gain sufficient support in the Council, nor was the problem solved by the 2024 directive defining offences and penalties. The change of government in Hungary and the review of EPPO’s mandate scheduled for 2026 offer an opportunity to revisit the issue.
articles.summary_zh: 制裁是欧盟外交与安全政策的关键工具，但其执行依赖于欧盟的法律和政治行动能力，而起诉仍受限于国家边界。为此，法国和德国于2022年提议扩大欧洲检察官办公室（EPPO）的职权，以涵盖违反欧盟制裁的行为。然而，该倡议未能在欧盟理事会获得足够支持，2024年关于定义犯罪和处罚的指令也未能解决问题。匈牙利政府更迭及EPPO职权审查（定于2026年）为重新讨论此议题提供了机会。文章认为，扩大EPPO职权至制裁执行领域，可增强欧盟制裁的有效性和一致性，克服当前国家层面的执行碎片化问题，从而强化欧盟的外交与安全政策工具。尽管面临政治和法律挑战，但这一改革对维护欧盟的全球信誉和战略自主至关重要。
subjects: Justice and Home Affairs in the EU

articles.title: China’s Emergence as a Volumetric State
articles.title_zh: 中国作为“体积国家”的崛起
articles.description: The Volumetric Dimension of the 15th Five-Year Plan and BeyondThe Chinese leadership has implemented various official directives, plans, and poli­cies that are, step-by-step, coalescing into a comprehensive architecture of volumetric statecraft. Volumetric power integrates spaces such as the atmosphere, the deep sea, the polar regions, the (geological) subsoil, or outer space into political governance. Volumetric states develop special economic and techn(olog)ical capacities to gain control of, and even govern, these spaces. Volumetric statecraft implies the Chinese state’s willingness and capacity to integrate various policy dimensions into a set of comprehensive ecosystems. China’s 15th Five-Year Plan specifically manifests the volumetric dimension in Chinese governance. This directly impacts the type of chal­lenge that China will pose to the European Union (EU) in the coming years, and it will force the EU to adapt a volumetric strategy of its own towards China.
articles.summary_zh: 文章指出，中国领导层正通过一系列官方指令、规划和政策，逐步构建起一套全面的“体积国家治理”架构。这种治理模式将大气层、深海、极地、地质底层和外层空间等立体空间纳入政治治理范畴。体积国家通过发展特殊的经济和技术能力，以获取甚至治理这些空间。中国的“体积国家治理”体现了国家将不同政策维度整合为综合生态系统的意愿和能力。即将出台的“十五五”规划将明确体现这一治理维度。文章认为，这种治理模式将直接影响中国在未来几年对欧盟构成的挑战类型，并迫使欧盟调整自身对华战略，发展一种对应的“体积战略”。
subjects: State and society of a country / a region
Economy and finances of a country / a region

articles.title: Cybersecurity Needs Secure Software
articles.title_zh: 网络安全需要安全软件
articles.description: How Policymakers Can Hold Software Vendors Accountable – and Why They ShouldCybersecurity incidents cause harm – for example, when adversarial states paralyse critical infrastructure or steal sensitive data. Many such incidents are only possible because many software products have known vulnerabilities. Software vendors could fix these, but they have little incentive to invest in the security of their products. To date, cybersecurity policy and protective measures have primarily addressed the symptoms of insecure software, rather than the root cause, namely software insecu­rity itself. This calls for regulation, specifically in the areas of product safety law, pro­duct liability regulations, and cybersecurity requirements for providers of software services. The European Union (EU) has already adopted initial rules, but regulatory gaps remain, and it is unclear whether member states will strictly enforce them. The German government should therefore now advocate for comprehensive European product liability regulations for software, and the Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) should impose fines on companies that violate existing rules.
articles.summary_zh: 本文指出，许多网络安全事件（如关键基础设施瘫痪或敏感数据泄露）之所以发生，是因为软件产品存在已知漏洞，而软件供应商缺乏投资于产品安全的动力。当前网络安全政策主要针对不安全软件的症状，而非根源——软件本身的不安全性。文章呼吁通过监管解决这一问题，具体领域包括产品安全法、产品责任法规以及对软件服务提供商的网络安全要求。欧盟已通过初步规则，但监管漏洞依然存在，且成员国是否严格执行尚不明确。因此，德国政府应推动制定全面的欧洲软件产品责任法规，并由联邦信息安全办公室对违反现有规定的企业处以罚款。文章强调，通过问责软件供应商，可以从根本上提升网络安全水平，减少因软件漏洞引发的攻击和数据泄露风险。

articles.title: India’s Space Policy
articles.title_zh: 印度的太空政策：利益、国际合作与相互冲突的目标
articles.description: Interests, International Cooperation and Conflicting GoalsThe Indian government is seeking to expand the country’s space programme in order to strengthen national security, promote economic growth and bolster India’s global standing. Its aim is to achieve strategic autonomy in the field of civilian space activities and technological sovereignty in the military sphere. In the area of security policy, India’s quest for sovereign capabilities is a race against time owing to its rivalry with China. But despite improved investment and regulatory conditions, there has not yet been any significant progress towards the development of a robust and competitive space economy. India’s most important strategic partner in the space sector is the US, with which it closely cooperates on trade, security and intelligence matters. In the field of civilian space activities, however, there is a growing interest in cooperation with European actors. India wants to attract more investment into the country, close technology gaps and diversify its partner­ships.
articles.summary_zh: 本文分析了印度太空政策的战略目标、发展现状及国际合作格局。印度政府正致力于扩大太空计划，以加强国家安全、促进经济增长并提升全球地位。在民用太空领域，印度追求战略自主；在军事领域，则寻求技术主权。由于与中国的竞争，印度在安全政策领域对主权能力的追求成为一场与时间的赛跑。尽管投资和监管环境有所改善，但印度在建立强大且具有竞争力的太空经济方面尚未取得显著进展。美国是印度在太空领域最重要的战略伙伴，双方在贸易、安全和情报方面密切合作。然而，在民用太空活动方面，印度对与欧洲行为体的合作兴趣日益增长，旨在吸引更多投资、缩小技术差距并实现伙伴关系多元化。文章指出，印度太空政策面临多重目标之间的内在冲突，包括安全自主与经济开放、技术主权与国际合作之间的平衡。
subjects: Defence and security policy / Armed forces
Foreign policy of a country / a region
Defence and security policy / armed forces of a country / a region
Economy and finances of a country / a region

articles.title: The Macroeconomics of Canadian Space
articles.title_zh: 加拿大太空经济的宏观视角
articles.description: Canada needs access to space. Over 20 percent of the Canadian economy is reliant on space capabilities; losing access to space could result in the loss of $1 billion per day for Canadians. But while many Canadians have a passing understanding of the role of space in economy and defence, it seems that neither the average Canadian nor the Canadian go
articles.summary_zh: 本文探讨了太空能力对加拿大经济与安全的战略重要性。文章指出，超过20%的加拿大经济依赖于太空基础设施，若失去太空接入，加拿大每天可能损失高达10亿加元。尽管许多加拿大人对太空在经济和国防中的作用有一定了解，但无论是普通民众还是政府决策者，似乎都未能充分认识到太空治理的紧迫性。文章强调，加拿大需要加强太空治理框架，以确保其在全球太空经济中的竞争力，并维护国家安全。作者呼吁加拿大政府采取更积极的政策，包括投资太空技术、加强国际合作，以及制定应对太空碎片和网络安全威胁的法规。文章认为，太空不仅是科学探索的前沿，更是现代经济与国防的基石，加拿大必须将其视为国家战略的核心组成部分。

articles.title: China’s Economy: Ambition and Challenges
articles.description: China is in debt. Its debt is nearly 300 percent of its GDP, the fourth highest among the G20. Nevertheless, China has set the ambitious goal of doubling its per capita income from 2020 levels by 2035. Significant challenges stand in its path: its rising debt, a shrinking labour force and growing protectionist pressures.CIGI Senior Fellow Mark Krug
articles.summary_zh: 本文探讨了中国当前面临的经济困境与未来目标之间的矛盾。文章指出，中国的债务水平已接近GDP的300%，在G20国家中排名第四高。尽管如此，中国仍设定了到2035年将人均收入从2020年水平翻一番的雄心勃勃目标。然而，实现这一目标面临三大挑战：不断攀升的债务负担、劳动力人口萎缩以及日益增长的贸易保护主义压力。作者Mark Krug作为CIGI高级研究员，分析了这些因素如何可能制约中国的经济增长潜力，并强调金融治理和贸易政策在应对这些挑战中的关键作用。文章认为，中国需要在维持经济增长与控制债务风险之间寻求平衡，同时应对全球贸易环境的变化。

articles.title: Beyond Harmony, Beyond Rhetoric: Politicizing Ubuntu for AI Governance
articles.title_zh: 超越和谐与修辞：将Ubuntu政治化以推动人工智能治理
articles.description: Ubuntu is often invoked as more than a language in AI ethics. It is treated as a relational African resource and as an African alternative to liberal individualism, signalling the presence of more careful and thoughtful ethical standards without the handling of harms, power and accountability in AI governance being actually any stronger. This rheto
articles.summary_zh: 本文探讨了Ubuntu（一种非洲哲学概念，强调人际关系与共同体）在人工智能伦理中的运用。作者指出，Ubuntu常被视作一种关系性非洲资源，以及自由主义个人主义的替代方案，暗示其能带来更谨慎、更具思考性的伦理标准。然而，文章批评这种修辞并未实际增强人工智能治理中对伤害、权力和问责的处理能力。作者主张，应超越表面和谐与空洞修辞，将Ubuntu政治化，即通过批判性审视其权力动态和实际应用，来推动更有效的AI治理框架。文章强调，仅依赖文化资源而不解决治理中的核心问题，可能导致伦理标准的空洞化。因此，需要将Ubuntu从抽象理念转化为具体政策工具，以应对AI带来的挑战，并确保问责机制的落实。这一分析揭示了AI伦理中文化挪用与治理实效之间的张力，呼吁更务实的治理路径。

articles.title: China’s Influence on Standards Development Organizations in the Digital Age
articles.title_zh: 数字时代中国对标准制定组织的影响力
articles.description: China’s influence on standard setting is growing but not in the way you might expect. Technologically, China is undeniably a powerhouse. However, its influence on key areas of international digital standardization is stunted, and CIGI Senior Fellow Alex He examines this gap in this paper. He identifies three perspectives from which China’s lag can
articles.summary_zh: 本文由CIGI高级研究员Alex He撰写，探讨了中国在国际数字标准化领域的影响力现状。尽管中国在技术层面无疑是强国，但在关键的国际数字标准化领域，其影响力却受到制约。文章从三个视角分析了中国在这一领域的滞后现象：一是中国在标准制定组织中的参与度和话语权有限；二是国际标准制定过程仍由西方主导，中国难以有效推动自身标准；三是中国国内标准体系与国际标准体系存在差异，导致协调困难。作者指出，中国虽在人工智能等变革性技术领域取得显著进展，但在全球标准制定中的实际影响力与科技实力并不匹配，这一差距值得关注。文章呼吁中国应更积极参与国际标准制定，以缩小这一鸿沟。

articles.title: Time for Canada to Take a Stand on Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems
articles.description: It is time: Canada must take a stand on lethal autonomous weapons systems (LAWS). So far, its stance has been understandably ambiguous. But if it is to stand with its stated position of ensuring meaningful human control — and if it is to uphold its legacy in humanitarian treaty-making — Canada must not only take a stand regarding the Convention on
articles.summary_zh: It is time: Canada must take a stand on lethal autonomous weapons systems (LAWS). So far, its stance has been understandably ambiguous. But if it is to stand with its stated position of ensuring meani

articles.title: China’s Central Bank Digital Currency 2.0 and the Future of Digital Finance
articles.title_zh: 中国央行数字货币2.0与数字金融的未来
articles.description: China is taking bold steps in integrating a digital yuan into its economy. The e-CNY is neither fully centralized nor decentralized; it goes beyond “digital cash” to become “digital deposit money,” with the ability to grow interest and a two-tiered approach that innovates while strengthening existing financial institutions.As a central bank digital
articles.summary_zh: 本文探讨了中国在将数字人民币（e-CNY）融入经济体系方面的重大举措。文章指出，e-CNY既非完全中心化也非去中心化，它超越了“数字现金”的概念，演变为“数字存款货币”，具备利息增长能力，并采用双层架构，在创新的同时强化了现有金融机构。作为央行数字货币，e-CNY通过技术治理和数字政策，重塑了金融监管与货币流通模式。文章分析了这一创新如何影响全球金融体系，并强调了其在数字经济和金融治理中的关键作用，预示了未来数字金融的发展方向。

articles.title: Colombia as a Leader in Climate and Energy Policy
articles.title_zh: 哥伦比亚：气候与能源政策的领导者
articles.description: Requirements for German and European CooperationUnder President Gustavo Petro, Colombia has positioned itself internationally as a leader in climate action and in pursuing a just transition away from fossil fuels. Among other things, Petro’s climate and energy policy relied on supply-side measures to limit the exploitation of fossil fuels – a unique approach among those countries in the Global South that, like Colombia, export fossil fuels. At the same time, Petro has had only limited success in implementing this agenda within his own country. With the election victory of the right-wing opposition candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, the future of this reform course is now being renegotiated. Whether the transformation in Colombia succeeds without jeopardising short-term economic stability, security of supply, and social cohesion is relevant for many countries that export fossil fuels and have only limited fiscal space. They are therefore closely watching whether, and under what conditions, Germany and the European Union (EU) provide support for Colombia’s transformation. Failure would have political implications beyond Colombia.
articles.summary_zh: 在古斯塔沃·佩特罗总统领导下，哥伦比亚在国际上将自己定位为气候行动和公正转型的领导者，其气候与能源政策依赖供给侧措施限制化石燃料开采，这在全球南方化石燃料出口国中独树一帜。然而，佩特罗在国内实施这一议程的成功有限。随着右翼反对派候选人阿贝拉尔多·德拉埃斯普列利亚的选举胜利，这一改革路线的未来正在重新谈判。哥伦比亚的转型能否在不危及短期经济稳定、供应安全和社会凝聚力的前提下成功，对许多化石燃料出口国且财政空间有限的国家具有重要参考意义。它们正密切关注德国和欧盟是否以及在何种条件下为哥伦比亚的转型提供支持。失败将产生超越哥伦比亚的政治影响。

articles.title: The failed coup and Turkey’s path towards autocracy
articles.title_zh: 未遂政变与土耳其走向威权之路
articles.description: On 15 July 2016, factions within the Turkish military attempted to overthrow President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan and the government. The coup failed due to resistance from the public, opposition from the political leadership and divisions within the army itself. For Erdoğan, this presented a valuable political opportunity. Citing the need to avert further danger, he initiated far-reaching purges across the state apparatus and began a fundamental restructuring of the political system. Purges and institutional restructuring under a state of emergency On 21 July 2016, Parliament approved a state of emergency declared by the government. It remained in force until mid-July 2018. Erdoğan used it to carry out comprehensive purges across the state apparatus with 32 emergency decrees. Around 130,000 public-sector employees were dismissed. By the end of 2016, the authorities had arrested around 125,000 people, including 36,000 to 40,000 who were placed in pre-trial detention – with 149 journalists among them. By the end of 2022, the number of arrests and detentions in connection with the coup attempt had risen to around 332,000, with 4,000 judges and prosecutors losing their posts. Thousands of private schools, universities and foundations were closed by decree. A further 131 media companies were shut down, including 45 newspapers, 16 television stations, 23 radio stations and 3 news agencies. The authorities also revoked the passports of tens of thousands of people.  Extensive repression and the resulting climate of fear significantly restricted political competition and public debate. In this environment, the government initiated a restructuring of Turkey’s institutions. The decisive step was the constitutional referendum held in April 2017, in which a narrow majority voted to introduce a presidential system that significantly expanded the president’s powers. International election observers reported irregularities and concluded that the referendum was not free or fair. The legitimacy of the result remained controversial, not least because the Supreme Electoral Committee subsequently admitted around two million improperly stamped ballots on election night, thereby pushing the referendum to a narrow victory. The constitutional amendment strengthened executive power and largely concentrated it in the hands of the president. He was given extensive executive and appointment powers, while Parliament’s oversight and veto rights were restricted. In addition, the growing influence of the executive has weakened judicial independence. Numerous political scientists initially classified Turkey as a “competitive authoritarian” regime, with more recent analyses increasingly assessing it as an “autocracy”. International governance indices document this development. Turkey’s score in the Liberal Democracy Index nearly halved within a few years. Freedom House downgraded Turkey from “Partly Free” to “Not Free” in 2018. Since the state of emergency, the World Justice Project’s Rule of Law Index has also shown significant deficits in the rule of law and judicial independence. The economic consequences of autocratisation in Turkey Autocratisation was accompanied by a significant erosion of the central institutional framework of the Turkish economy. The weakening of democratic institutions, the rule of law and the protection of property rights, as well as the loss of central bank independence, increased uncertainties about economic policy. The growing concentration of power and the dismantling of institutional constraints on government action have negatively impacted the predictability of economic policy. This was reflected in key economic indicators, which pointed to a marked economic slowdown from 2016 onwards. In the first five years following the coup attempt, average annual real GDP growth fell from 6.5 in the preceding five-year period to 3.5 per cent, despite expansionary monetary and fiscal policies. At the same time, nominal GDP per capita fell from around $11,000 to $9,700. Foreign direct investment inflows also declined from an average of $15 billion to $11 billion. Export growth slowed from 5 to 3 per cent, while inflation rose from 7 to 14 per cent. The Turkish lira also depreciated significantly and became more volatile. There are currently no signs of a sustained economic recovery. The ongoing repression against the opposition party CHP and its mayors, as well as the government’s renewed efforts to amend the constitution to secure its hold on power, are increasing political instability and impacting the investment climate.
articles.summary_zh: 2016年7月15日，土耳其军方部分势力试图推翻总统埃尔多安及政府，但因公众抵抗、政治领导层反对及军队内部分歧而失败。埃尔多安借此机会，以防范进一步危险为由，启动大规模国家机构清洗和政治体系根本性重组。紧急状态持续至2018年7月，期间约13万名公职人员被解职，33.2万人被捕或拘留，数千家媒体、学校及基金会被关闭。2017年宪法公投以微弱多数通过总统制，大幅扩大总统权力，但国际观察员指出公投存在违规，合法性受质疑。此后，行政权力集中削弱议会监督和司法独立，土耳其被政治学者从“竞争性威权”逐步归类为“威权政体”。威权化伴随经济制度框架侵蚀：民主制度、法治、产权保护及央行独立性弱化，导致经济政策不确定性上升。政变后五年，年均实际GDP增长率从6.5%降至3.5%，人均名义GDP从约1.1万美元降至9700美元，外国直接投资从150亿美元降至110亿美元，出口增长放缓，通胀从7%升至14%，里拉大幅贬值。当前，对反对党及市长的持续镇压、政府修宪巩固权力的努力，进一步加剧政治不稳定，影响投资环境。文章核心观点是，未遂政变成为土耳其加速威权化的转折点，并带来严重经济后果。
subjects: State and society of a country / a region
Defence and security policy / armed forces of a country / a region

articles.title: Southeast Asia – More Than Just a Side Issue in the Indo-Pacific
articles.title_zh: 东南亚：印太地区不容忽视的关键角色
articles.description: The Strategic Importance of the Region for German Foreign PolicyThe Federal President’s trip in mid-June 2026, which took him to Indonesia and the Philippines, among other destinations, has once again signalled the growing awareness within German foreign policy of the importance of Southeast Asia. However, when it comes to Germany’s relationship with the Indo-Pacific, Northeast Asia and India have so far received greater attention. Relations with Southeast Asia remain comparatively underdeveloped and driven by economic interests, with the primary aim of reducing dependence on China. Beyond its economic potential, however, the region has strategic significance in its own right: as an arena of great-power rivalry, a hub for global supply chains, and an increasingly vocal presence in multilateral forums. To strengthen its long-term interests in stability and a rules-based internatio­nal order in the Indo-Pacific, Germany must deepen its cooperation with Southeast Asia – particularly as numerous actors are competing for influence there.
articles.summary_zh: 2026年6月中旬，德国联邦总统访问印尼和菲律宾等地，标志着德国外交政策对东南亚重要性的认识日益增强。然而，德国在印太地区的关系中，东北亚和印度迄今受到更多关注，与东南亚的关系仍相对欠发达，且主要受经济利益驱动，核心目标是减少对中国的依赖。文章指出，东南亚不仅具有经济潜力，更拥有独立的战略意义：它是大国竞争的舞台、全球供应链的枢纽，并在多边论坛中声音日益响亮。为维护印太地区的稳定和基于规则的国际秩序，德国必须深化与东南亚的合作，尤其是在众多行为体竞相争夺该地区影响力的背景下。文章强调，德国应超越单纯的经济考量，将东南亚视为印太战略中不可或缺的支柱。
subjects: Foreign policy of a country / a region
Economy and finances of a country / a region

articles.title: The failed coup and Turkey’s path towards autocracy
articles.title_zh: 未遂政变与土耳其走向威权之路
articles.description: On 15 July 2016, factions within the Turkish military attempted to overthrow President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan and the government. The coup failed due to resistance from the public, opposition from the political leadership and divisions within the army itself. For Erdoğan, this presented a valuable political opportunity. Citing the need to avert further danger, he initiated far-reaching purges across the state apparatus and began a fundamental restructuring of the political system. Purges and institutional restructuring under a state of emergency On 21 July 2016, Parliament approved a state of emergency declared by the government. It remained in force until mid-July 2018. Erdoğan used it to carry out comprehensive purges across the state apparatus with 32 emergency decrees. Around 130,000 public-sector employees were dismissed. By the end of 2016, the authorities had arrested around 125,000 people, including 36,000 to 40,000 who were placed in pre-trial detention – with 149 journalists among them. By the end of 2022, the number of arrests and detentions in connection with the coup attempt had risen to around 332,000, with 4,000 judges and prosecutors losing their posts. Thousands of private schools, universities and foundations were closed by decree. A further 131 media companies were shut down, including 45 newspapers, 16 television stations, 23 radio stations and 3 news agencies. The authorities also revoked the passports of tens of thousands of people.  Extensive repression and the resulting climate of fear significantly restricted political competition and public debate. In this environment, the government initiated a restructuring of Turkey’s institutions. The decisive step was the constitutional referendum held in April 2017, in which a narrow majority voted to introduce a presidential system that significantly expanded the president’s powers. International election observers reported irregularities and concluded that the referendum was not free or fair. The legitimacy of the result remained controversial, not least because the Supreme Electoral Committee subsequently admitted around two million improperly stamped ballots on election night, thereby pushing the referendum to a narrow victory. The constitutional amendment strengthened executive power and largely concentrated it in the hands of the president. He was given extensive executive and appointment powers, while Parliament’s oversight and veto rights were restricted. In addition, the growing influence of the executive has weakened judicial independence. Numerous political scientists initially classified Turkey as a “competitive authoritarian” regime, with more recent analyses increasingly assessing it as an “autocracy”. International governance indices document this development. Turkey’s score in the Liberal Democracy Index nearly halved within a few years. Freedom House downgraded Turkey from “Partly Free” to “Not Free” in 2018. Since the state of emergency, the World Justice Project’s Rule of Law Index has also shown significant deficits in the rule of law and judicial independence. The economic consequences of autocratisation in Turkey Autocratisation was accompanied by a significant erosion of the central institutional framework of the Turkish economy. The weakening of democratic institutions, the rule of law and the protection of property rights, as well as the loss of central bank independence, increased uncertainties about economic policy. The growing concentration of power and the dismantling of institutional constraints on government action have negatively impacted the predictability of economic policy. This was reflected in key economic indicators, which pointed to a marked economic slowdown from 2016 onwards. In the first five years following the coup attempt, average annual real GDP growth fell from 6.5 in the preceding five-year period to 3.5 per cent, despite expansionary monetary and fiscal policies. At the same time, nominal GDP per capita fell from around $11,000 to $9,700. Foreign direct investment inflows also declined from an average of $15 billion to $11 billion. Export growth slowed from 5 to 3 per cent, while inflation rose from 7 to 14 per cent. The Turkish lira also depreciated significantly and became more volatile. There are currently no signs of a sustained economic recovery. The ongoing repression against the opposition party CHP and its mayors, as well as the government’s renewed efforts to amend the constitution to secure its hold on power, are increasing political instability and impacting the investment climate.
articles.summary_zh: 2016年7月15日，土耳其军方部分派系试图推翻总统埃尔多安及政府，但因公众抵抗、政治领导层反对及军队内部分歧而失败。埃尔多安利用这一机会，以防范进一步危险为由，在国家机构内发起大规模清洗，并开始根本性的政治体系重组。紧急状态持续至2018年7月，期间通过32项紧急法令解雇约13万名公职人员，逮捕约33.2万人，关闭数千所私立学校、大学及媒体机构。2017年宪法公投以微弱优势通过总统制，大幅扩大总统权力，国际观察员指出公投存在违规。此后，行政权力集中削弱了司法独立，土耳其被政治学家归类为“竞争性威权”甚至“威权”政权。威权化伴随经济制度框架的侵蚀，民主制度、法治、产权保护及央行独立性受损，导致经济政策不确定性增加。2016年后五年平均GDP增长率从6.5%降至3.5%，人均名义GDP从约1.1万美元降至9700美元，外国直接投资减少，通胀率从7%升至14%，里拉大幅贬值。当前对反对党及其市长的持续镇压及修宪努力加剧政治不稳定，影响投资环境。文章核心观点是，未遂政变成为土耳其加速威权化的转折点，并对其经济产生了显著的负面后果。
